calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emily Guevara for (petitioner) Lisa Chiu who is present in court.

The Court stated that there is an issue with the 2 properties that will pass by right of survivorship and not be part of the estate. Ms. Guevara stated that they would like those properties confirmed for tax purposes. The Court stated that it cannot control how the IRS views things, that it is not inclined to reach out regarding property that is not estate property, that the Vicente property was held by the decedent as his separate property, that the heirs suggest it was purchased with community assets and that there have been no objections. Ms. Guevara stated that the Vicente property was purchased during the marriage with joint funds and that there was never a transmutation to separate property. The Court stated that it does not understand how it helped the niece to have the property in his separate name. Ms. Guevara stated that there are family dynamics between the niece and her husband, that the decedent agreed to hold it as separate property but that between Ms. Guevara's client and husband it was agreed that it was community property. The petition for spousal property was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dai for (Petitioner) Lisa Chiu.

The Court stated that it needed a response to the examiner's notes. Mr. Dai stated that he filed an amended petition on 4/28/15 and that he did not receive notes after that date. The Petition for Spousal Property was continued to June 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Dai was directed to contact the examiner for clarification on what is needed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
